rows.description: Computus Gu: Paynter Rectory a secundo die
rows.notes: Top line of page title, fully legible script across top. No monetary columns on this line.
Second line of page title / date; clear handwriting and no amounts. Kept as a separate header line per page guidance.

rows.description: Imprimis reddidit comput: de remanentib: in lista 11 9 46
rows.notes: Guidance-specified header that contains numbers; numbers transcribed as part of the header text rather than as amounts. Text and digits are clear.
Section header introducing the arrears list; no amounts for the header itself.
Clear vertical alignment with ruling lines; pounds/shillings/pence read as 60, 0, 0 with unambiguous digits and all values valid.
Clear reading of '76 13 4' aligned to the pounds/shillings/pence columns (digits well-formed; all values in valid ranges).
Clear '20 0 0' in columns; handwriting and alignment unambiguous.
Amount '30 0 0' is crisply aligned. The small handwritten annotation 'pro mici' appears to the right of the place name; included parenthetically as an annotation per page guidance.
Place name is legible but there is no visible amount on this line; left blank intentionally. Vertical alignment shows empty amount columns for this row.

Digits read as '03 00 8'. There is a small trailing mark after the pence that could be a fraction symbol (loop/ob or q) but it is not distinct; chosen interpretation: whole pence = 8, fraction left blank. Alignment to the ruling lines is consistent for pounds/shillings/pence.
Name legible; no amount visible for this line (amount columns are blank at this vertical position).
Per page-specific guidance this row functions as a section header. Although there is numeric material nearby, this line is being treated as a header with no transactional amounts.
Amount reads clearly as '02 15 7' aligned to the columns immediately to the right of this entry. Handwriting of numerals is distinct.
Name legible. No clear amount appears on this row; the nearest amounts are associated with subsequent lines. Left blank to reflect visual evidence.
Numeric group reads '03 8 11' and aligns with the Bampton row. All values are within valid ranges and digits are legible.
Clear '04 0 0' aligned to this row; numerals unambiguous.
Amount reads '01 12 0' and is well aligned with the Hughes entry.
Clear '00 10 0' in the shilling column aligned to Greeneway; digits and alignment are distinct.
Amount reads '00 15 0' aligned with Walker. Handwriting clear and values valid.
Place name legible; no amount is visible on the same horizontal line, so left blank.
Entry visible but no numeric value appears aligned on this line; left blank.
Name legible; amount column empty at this vertical position. Could be a small gap intentionally left for this row.

This is the first (bracketed) line of the Tintinhull pair. The numeric group is faint/patchy and appears as '02 5 5' with an additional faint mark to the right. I interpret the main digits as £2, 5s, 5d; the trailing mark is ambiguous (could be a stray ink mark or fraction), so fraction left blank. Per page guidance these braced items are separate entries and each has its own amount.
Second braced Tintinhull line (Hopkins). Amount clearly reads '01 18 7' and is well aligned. Treated as an independent entry per page guidance.
Numeric '03 19 8' aligns with this row; digits are clear and within valid ranges.
Amount reads '01 15 6' in columns next to this entry. Alignment and numerals are legible.

There is a sequence of three numbers near the bottom ('03 19 8', '01 15 6', '06 9 7'). The middle value '01 15 6' could belong to Bensington or Brew house depending on exact vertical alignment; I assigned '01 15 6' to Brew house as one plausible alignment but alignment is moderately ambiguous. Chosen reading due to approximate visual spacing; confidence lowered accordingly.
Final numeric in the lower group reads '06 9 7' and visually aligns with Trethewin/Trethewin row. Digits are clear.
Entry text legible; no amount is visible on the same horizontal line (columns appear blank here).
Final subtotal clearly written as '230 13 8' and used as alignment reference for the pounds/shillings/pence ruling lines as instructed. Values are unambiguous.
